instruction: A CS student answered these questions: loves building websites, spends free time on side projects, wants to build a SaaS product, loses time making UI pixel-perfect, prefers startup environment, finds mobile apps fascinating, loves great UI/UX, would finish a Web Dev Bootcamp, skill level is beginner, knows Git. Analyze this profile and determine the best career domain.
output:
DOMAIN: Full Stack Web Development
CONFIDENCE: High
REASONING: The student consistently shows visual/product orientation — pixel-perfect UI obsession, SaaS dream, Web Dev Bootcamp preference. Side project habit at beginner level is a strong positive signal. Mobile interest suggests frontend/product mindset. Git knowledge shows readiness to enter collaborative development workflow.
SKILL_LEVEL: beginner
INFERRED_INTERESTS: Web Development, UI/UX, Frontend, Mobile Apps, JavaScript
NEXT_STEP: Start with HTML/CSS/JS fundamentals immediately. Build one project per month.

instruction: A CS student answered these questions: fascinated by hacking and security, plays with Docker/cloud in free time, reverse-engineers systems, prefers security team environment, Kubernetes/Terraform interests, systems thinking coder, would finish Ethical Hacking course, loves talking about IoT edge computing, inspired by bug bounty researcher, intermediate skill level, knows both Git and Docker. Analyze this profile.
output:
DOMAIN: Cybersecurity
CONFIDENCE: High
REASONING: Overwhelming security signal across multiple question types — hacking fascination, reverse-engineering enjoyment, security career inspiration, and course preference all point to Cybersecurity. Docker/Kubernetes knowledge and systems thinking suggest DevOps is a secondary path but security is primary. Bug bounty inspiration is a decisive signal.
SKILL_LEVEL: intermediate
INFERRED_INTERESTS: Cybersecurity, Ethical Hacking, Networking, Linux, Penetration Testing
NEXT_STEP: Start TryHackMe immediately. Target CompTIA Security+ in 6 months.

instruction: A student answered: hooked on data and patterns, reads ML papers in free time, wants to build an AI assistant, obsessed with model improvement, prefers research lab, LLMs fascinate them, algorithms-focused coder, would finish Python ML Bootcamp, discusses AI future, inspired by ML engineer, advanced skill level, knows Git and SQL. Analyze this profile.
output:
DOMAIN: Machine Learning & AI
CONFIDENCE: Very High
REASONING: Near-perfect ML signal alignment — ML paper reading, AI assistant dream project, model optimization obsession, research lab preference, LLM fascination, and algorithmic thinking are all tier-1 ML indicators. Advanced level with Git/SQL foundation makes them ready for PyTorch and deep learning immediately. Research inspiration suggests they may lean toward AI Research & NLP as a sub-specialization.
SKILL_LEVEL: advanced
INFERRED_INTERESTS: Machine Learning, Deep Learning, NLP, Research, LLM, AI/ML
NEXT_STEP: Start with PyTorch, then move to Hugging Face transformers. Target a Kaggle competition.

instruction: A student answered: loves video games and wonders how they're built, tinkers with Unity in free time, wants to build a multiplayer online game, loses time on game physics problems, prefers gaming studio, interested in rendering engines, thinks in game mechanics, would finish a Unity course, fascinated by AAA game development, inspired by indie dev success, intermediate level, knows Git. Analyze this profile.
output:
DOMAIN: Game Development
CONFIDENCE: Very High
REASONING: Every single answer points to Game Development — gaming origin story, Unity tinkering, multiplayer game dream project, game physics obsession, gaming studio preference, rendering engine interest, AAA discussion preference, and indie dev inspiration. This is the clearest domain signal possible. Unity with C# is the correct starting point.
SKILL_LEVEL: intermediate
INFERRED_INTERESTS: Game Development, Unity, 3D, Gaming, Game Design
NEXT_STEP: Complete Brackeys Unity series, then build and ship a game on itch.io.

instruction: A student: fascinated by blockchain after reading about DeFi, builds smart contracts in free time, wants to build a DeFi protocol, gets excited by cryptography problems, prefers Web3 startup, Solidity fascinates them, thinks experimentally, would finish a Solidity course, discusses tokenomics at meetups, inspired by DeFi protocol founder, intermediate skill level, knows Git. Analyze.
output:
DOMAIN: Blockchain Development
CONFIDENCE: High
REASONING: Strong blockchain signal — DeFi fascination, smart contract building, DeFi dream project, cryptography interest, Web3 startup preference, and Solidity focus are all clear domain indicators. Tokenomics discussion preference and DeFi founder inspiration confirm. The experimental coding style fits well with the iterative nature of smart contract development.
SKILL_LEVEL: intermediate
INFERRED_INTERESTS: Blockchain, Web3, DeFi, Smart Contracts, Solidity
NEXT_STEP: Master Solidity on Hardhat, deploy an ERC-20 token on testnet.

instruction: A student: got into CS through curiosity about Linux and servers, plays with Raspberry Pi and Docker in free time, wants to build a fully automated CI/CD infrastructure, loves optimizing slow pipelines, prefers platform/infrastructure team, Kubernetes and Terraform fascinate, systems-oriented coder, would finish AWS certification course, loves talking about cloud-native architecture, inspired by SRE at Google, advanced level, knows Git and Docker. Analyze.
output:
DOMAIN: Cloud & DevOps Engineering
CONFIDENCE: Very High
REASONING: Textbook DevOps/Cloud profile — Linux origins, Raspberry Pi + Docker hobby, CI/CD dream project, pipeline optimization enjoyment, infrastructure team preference, Kubernetes/Terraform fascination, AWS certification intent, cloud-native discussion preference, and SRE inspiration. Advanced level with Git and Docker already in hand means they are ready to move directly into Kubernetes and IaC.
SKILL_LEVEL: advanced
INFERRED_INTERESTS: Cloud, DevOps, Kubernetes, CI/CD, AWS, Terraform, Infrastructure
NEXT_STEP: Get CKA certified. Deploy a production-grade app on AWS EKS with Terraform.

instruction: A student: got into CS through love of databases and SQL, spends free time building ETL scripts, dreams of building a real-time data pipeline for millions of events, loves optimizing slow queries, prefers data platform team, Apache Kafka fascinates, performance-oriented coder, would finish a Data Engineering bootcamp, talks about data warehousing at meetups, inspired by data engineer at Uber, intermediate level, knows Git and SQL. Analyze.
output:
DOMAIN: Data Engineering
CONFIDENCE: High
REASONING: Clear Data Engineering profile — SQL origin, ETL hobby, real-time pipeline dream, query optimization enjoyment, data platform team preference, Kafka fascination, and Uber data engineer inspiration are all strong signals. SQL knowledge confirmed. The performance-oriented coding style matches data engineering well. Should learn Apache Spark next.
SKILL_LEVEL: intermediate
INFERRED_INTERESTS: Data Engineering, SQL, Big Data, ETL, Databases, Kafka
NEXT_STEP: Complete DataTalks Data Engineering Zoomcamp. Build an end-to-end pipeline with Airflow + Spark.

instruction: A student: got into CS through tinkering with Arduino, spends free time building IoT sensors, wants to build a smart home automation system, loves low-level hardware programming, prefers embedded systems team at automotive company, RTOS and microcontrollers fascinate, thinks in terms of memory and registers, would finish Embedded C course, discusses edge computing at meetups, inspired by firmware engineer at Tesla, intermediate level, knows Git. Analyze.
output:
DOMAIN: Embedded Systems & IoT
CONFIDENCE: Very High
REASONING: Near-perfect Embedded/IoT signal — Arduino origins, IoT hobby, smart home dream, hardware programming obsession, automotive embedded preference, RTOS/microcontroller fascination, memory-register thinking, Embedded C course choice, edge computing interest, and Tesla firmware inspiration are definitive. This student should pursue ARM microcontrollers, FreeRTOS, and eventually Linux kernel modules.
SKILL_LEVEL: intermediate
INFERRED_INTERESTS: Embedded Systems, IoT, Hardware, Arduino, RTOS, Robotics
NEXT_STEP: Master STM32 with FreeRTOS. Build a complete IoT sensor network project.

instruction: A student: got into CS through mobile apps, builds Android apps in free time, wants to build a super-app for India, loses time perfecting app animations, prefers product startup, Flutter fascinates, UI-focused coder, would finish Flutter bootcamp, discusses app monetization at meetups, inspired by developer who built a viral app, beginner level, knows nothing yet. Analyze.
output:
DOMAIN: Mobile App Development
CONFIDENCE: High

Beginner level means they should start with Dart/Flutter fundamentals before moving to state management and Firebase integration.

instruction: A student: got into CS through reading research papers, spends free time reading NLP papers and implementing them, wants to build a system that understands human language perfectly, loves improving model accuracy and benchmarks, prefers AI research lab, transformer architectures fascinate, thinks mathematically, would audit Stanford CS224N, discusses NLP breakthroughs at meetups, inspired by research scientist at DeepMind, advanced level, knows Git and Python. output:
DOMAIN: AI Research & NLP
CONFIDENCE: Very High

Python and Git foundation is solid. Should move immediately to Hugging Face, fine-tuning BERT, and eventually publishing on arXiv.

instruction: A student gives mixed signals: interested in both web development and data science, builds web apps but also runs Jupyter notebooks, wants to build either a data dashboard or a full-stack product, intermediate level. What should they do?
output:
DOMAIN: Full Stack Web Development
CONFIDENCE: Medium
REASONING: Mixed signals between Web Development and Data Science. However, web development is slightly stronger due to the app-building behavior and full-stack product goal. Recommend Full Stack Web Development as the primary path with Data visualization as a specialty — building data dashboards is a natural intersection. Tools like D3.js, Chart.js, and connecting to Python data APIs will satisfy both interests.
SKILL_LEVEL: intermediate
INFERRED_INTERESTS: Web Development, Data Science, Frontend, Visualization
NEXT_STEP: Build a full-stack data dashboard app — React frontend + Python FastAPI backend + PostgreSQL.

instruction: A first-year student with no experience, no languages known, not sure what they like, but thinks security and hacking sounds cool from movies, and also thinks AI is interesting. Beginner. What path?
output:
DOMAIN: Cybersecurity
CONFIDENCE: Medium
REASONING: For a complete beginner drawn to security, starting with Cybersecurity fundamentals is valid — Linux, networking, and Python are transferable to any domain. The hacking interest, even if movie-inspired, often converts into genuine passion once students try platforms like TryHackMe. AI is a secondary interest that can be revisited after building programming fundamentals. Security provides clearer beginner milestones through certifications like CompTIA Security+.
SKILL_LEVEL: beginner
INFERRED_INTERESTS: Cybersecurity, Linux, Networking
NEXT_STEP: Install Kali Linux in VirtualBox. Start TryHackMe free path. Learn Python basics in parallel.

instruction: Generate a 6-month learning milestone for a beginner Full Stack Web Development student.
output:
MILESTONE: Month 1-2 — HTML, CSS & JavaScript Foundations
SKILLS: HTML5 semantic markup, CSS Flexbox/Grid, Vanilla JavaScript ES6+, DOM manipulation, responsive design
PROJECTS: Personal portfolio website, responsive landing page clone
RESOURCES: freeCodeCamp HTML & CSS Full Course (YouTube), The Odin Project (free), JavaScript.info
MILESTONE: Month 3-4 — React & Frontend Frameworks
SKILLS: React.js, hooks, state management, React Router, component architecture
PROJECTS: Todo app with React, weather dashboard using OpenWeather API
RESOURCES: React official docs, Scrimba React course, Programming with Mosh React tutorial
MILESTONE: Month 5-6 — Backend & Full Stack
SKILLS: Node.js, Express.js, REST API design, MongoDB, JWT authentication
PROJECTS: Blog API with CRUD operations, user authentication system
RESOURCES: Traversy Media Node.js crash course, freeCodeCamp MongoDB tutorial

instruction: Generate a 6-month learning milestone for an intermediate Machine Learning student.
output:
MILESTONE: Month 1-2 — Deep Learning Foundations
SKILLS: Neural networks, backpropagation, CNNs, PyTorch framework, GPU training
PROJECTS: Image classifier on CIFAR-10, custom CNN from scratch
RESOURCES: fast.ai Practical Deep Learning, Andrej Karpathy Neural Nets from Scratch (YouTube)
MILESTONE: Month 3-4 — NLP & Transformers
SKILLS: Attention mechanism, BERT, GPT architecture, Hugging Face ecosystem, fine-tuning
PROJECTS: Text sentiment classifier, named entity recognizer, custom chatbot
RESOURCES: Hugging Face NLP course (free), Stanford CS224N lectures (YouTube)
MILESTONE: Month 5-6 — MLOps & Production
SKILLS: MLflow experiment tracking, FastAPI model serving, Docker containerization, AWS SageMaker basics
PROJECTS: End-to-end ML pipeline, deployed model API with monitoring
RESOURCES: Made With ML MLOps course, freeCodeCamp MLOps YouTube

instruction: What are the most important skills a Cybersecurity student should learn in their first year?
output:
PRIORITY_SKILLS for Year 1 Cybersecurity:
1. Linux CLI mastery — everything in security runs on Linux. Learn file permissions, networking commands, process management.
2. Networking fundamentals — TCP/IP, DNS, HTTP/S, firewalls, subnetting. Cannot hack what you don't understand.
3. Python scripting — automate tasks, write exploits, build tools. Most security tools are Python.
4. OWASP Top 10 — the bible of web vulnerabilities. SQL injection, XSS, CSRF, SSRF — know them deeply.
5. Nmap & Wireshark — the two tools every security person uses daily.
6. TryHackMe — complete the Jr Penetration Tester path (free). Hands-on is non-negotiable.
7. Git — version control your scripts and tools.
AVOID: Jumping to Metasploit before understanding what it does manually. Tools before concepts is a trap.

instruction: A student completed the questionnaire and got recommended Cloud & DevOps but their dream is to work at Google. What specific path should they take?
output:
GOOGLE-TARGETED Cloud & DevOps Path:
Google hires for SRE (Site Reliability Engineering) and Cloud Engineer roles. Here is the specific path:
Year 1: Master Linux deeply (LPIC-1), learn Go programming (Google's primary language), complete GCP Associate Cloud Engineer certification.
Year 2: Kubernetes CKA certification (Google created Kubernetes), Terraform Associate, contribute to a CNCF open source project on GitHub.
Year 3: System design — study 'Designing Data-Intensive Applications' by Kleppmann. Practice distributed systems problems. Complete Google's SRE book (free online).
Final push: Apply to Google's STEP internship (2nd/3rd year) or APM program. LeetCode 150 medium problems minimum for the coding interview.
KEY DIFFERENTIATOR: Open source contributions to Kubernetes ecosystem. Google SRE interviewers love candidates who've touched real distributed systems.

instruction: Compare career prospects between Full Stack Web Development and Cloud & DevOps for a student graduating in 2 years in India.
output:
COMPARISON for Indian job market, 2-year timeline:
FULL STACK WEB DEVELOPMENT:
- Entry salary: ₹5-8 LPA at service companies, ₹8-15 LPA at product startups
- Demand: Very high — every company needs web developers
- Time to first job: 6-12 months of focused learning
- Hot skills: React, Node.js, TypeScript, PostgreSQL
- Top hirers: Razorpay, Zepto, Swiggy, Meesho, Groww
CLOUD & DEVOPS:
- Entry salary: ₹7-12 LPA at service companies, ₹12-22 LPA at product companies
- Demand: High and growing faster than web dev
- Time to first job: 12-18 months (more to learn)
- Hot skills: AWS, Kubernetes, Terraform, Python/Go
- Top hirers: Juspay, Razorpay, Flipkart, AWS India
RECOMMENDATION: If you need a job in 6-12 months → Full Stack. If you can invest 12-18 months → DevOps pays better long-term. Both are excellent choices in India's current market.

instruction: A student knows Python and has done some data analysis. They are in 3rd year B.Tech. Should they go into Data Engineering or Machine Learning?
output:
RECOMMENDATION: Machine Learning first, then specialize.
REASONING: Python + data analysis background is the perfect ML starting point. Data Engineering requires the same Python foundation but diverges into SQL, Spark, and pipeline tooling — which is less exciting than ML for most students and harder to self-learn without real data infrastructure to practice on.
ML PATH from their current position:
- Already know: Python, data analysis (pandas/numpy assumed)
- Next 3 months: Scikit-learn, statistics, first Kaggle competition
- Next 6 months: PyTorch, CNNs, build 2 portfolio projects
- Final year: Specialize in NLP or CV, target ML Engineer roles
DATA ENGINEERING alternative: Better suited if they prefer infrastructure over modeling, enjoy SQL deeply, and want to work on data pipelines rather than algorithms. ₹6-10 LPA entry in India.
VERDICT: ML is more exciting, has higher ceiling, and Python background makes the transition natural. Data Engineering is the safer, more structured path.

instruction: What Git and Docker skills are mandatory for every CS student regardless of their domain?
output:
MANDATORY TOOLS — Non-negotiable for every developer in every domain:
GIT & GITHUB (Week 1-2 of any learning path):
- git init, add, commit, push, pull
- Branching: git branch, checkout, merge, rebase
- GitHub: Pull Requests, Issues, GitHub Actions basics
- .gitignore, README.md, semantic commit messages
- WHY: Every technical interview will assume Git knowledge. Every job requires it. No exceptions.
DOCKER (Month 2-3 of any learning path):
- docker build, run, ps, exec, logs
- Dockerfile writing — FROM, RUN, COPY, EXPOSE, CMD
- docker-compose for multi-service apps
- Container networking and volumes
- WHY: Modern software runs in containers. DevOps, ML model serving, web apps — all use Docker. Hiring managers filter for this.
GITHUB ACTIONS (Month 4-6):
- Basic CI/CD pipeline — run tests on every push
- Automated Docker builds
- Deploy on push to main
TIMELINE: Git in week 1 (non-negotiable). Docker in month 2-3. GitHub Actions in month 4-6. In that order.

instruction: A student is in final year with only 8 months left. They have basic Python knowledge. What is the fastest path to getting a job?
output:
FAST-TRACK 8-MONTH JOB PLAN (Python background):
Month 1-2: Pick ONE domain immediately. Best options with Python background: ML Engineer, Data Scientist, Backend Developer, or DevOps (Python+). Do not explore multiple paths.
Month 3-4: Build 2 portfolio projects in that domain. Host everything on GitHub. Make README files professional. Deploy at least one project publicly.
Month 5: Start applying. Do not wait until you feel 'ready'. Apply to 5 companies per day. Target: startups (3-50 person companies hire faster than MNCs).
Month 6: DSA preparation — 50 LeetCode easy/medium problems. Most startup interviews are practical but some ask DSA.
Month 7-8: Interview sprint — mock interviews, system design basics, behavioral questions (STAR format).
PRIORITY STACK for fastest Indian job market entry with Python:
1. Django/FastAPI + PostgreSQL → Backend Developer (₹5-10 LPA)
2. Pandas + Scikit-learn + one visualization tool → Data Analyst (₹4-8 LPA)
3. AWS basics + Docker → Cloud/DevOps Junior (₹6-12 LPA)
CRITICAL: A deployed project beats a certificate every single time.
